title: Understanding Quantifiers in Logic
description: A visual explanation of universal and existential quantifiers, their symbols, meanings, and relationships to logical statements.
relationships: ∀x P(x) is true if P(x) is true for ALL elements in the domain
∃x P(x) is true if P(x) is true for AT LEAST ONE element in the domain
Quantifiers bind the variable x to the predicate P(x)
The domain defines the set of objects over which x ranges
